reason: The remediation modifies only the pam_pwquality 'difok' parameter in /etc/security/pwquality.conf, which is a standard authentication hardening setting. Changing this value does not affect system boot, networking, or filesystem layout and carries minimal risk of lockout when set to a reasonable number.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Setting difok too high could prevent users from changing passwords; Potential user inconvenience if policy becomes overly strict
